boardClaim.desc: This will promote your user to instance admin and migrate company ownership access from local trusted mode.

invite.unavailableDesc: This invite may be expired, revoked, or already used.
invite.bootstrapDesc: The first instance admin is now configured. You can continue to the board.
invite.joinRequestDesc: Your request is pending admin approval. You will not have access until approved.
invite.signInNote: Sign in or create an account before submitting a human join request.

agentDetail.logLoadFailed: Failed to load workspace operation log
agentDetail.pendingApprovalWarning: This agent is pending board approval and cannot be invoked yet.
agentDetail.skillsDescription: Skills are reusable instruction bundles the agent can invoke from its local tool environment. This view shows the current instructions file and the skills currently visible to the local agent runtime.
agentDetail.noInstructionsFile: No instructions file configured for this agent.
agentDetail.resumeSkipped: Resume request was skipped because the agent is not currently invokable.
agentDetail.retrySkipped: Retry was skipped because the agent is not currently invokable.
agentDetail.sessionClearConfirm: Clear session for {{count}} issue(s) touched by this run?
agentDetail.transcriptNoPersisted: No persisted transcript for this run.
agentDetail.keyCreatedBanner: API key created — copy it now, it will not be shown again.
agentDetail.createApiKeyDesc: API keys allow this agent to authenticate calls to the Paperclip server.
agentDetail.canCreateAgentsHint: Allow this agent to create new agents in the system.

routines.selectCompany: Select a company to view routines.
routines.title: Routines
routines.subtitle: Recurring work definitions that materialize into auditable execution issues.
routines.newRoutineDesc: Define the recurring work first. Trigger setup comes next on the detail page.
routines.advancedDeliveryDesc: Keep policy controls secondary to the work definition.
routines.coalesceIfActiveDesc: If a run is already active, keep just one follow-up run queued.
routines.alwaysEnqueueDesc: Queue every trigger occurrence, even if the routine is already running.
routines.skipIfActiveDesc: Drop new trigger occurrences while a run is still active.
routines.skipMissedDesc: Ignore windows that were missed while the scheduler or routine was paused.
routines.enqueueMissedWithCapDesc: Catch up missed schedule windows in capped batches after recovery.
routines.afterCreation: After creation, Paperclip takes you straight to trigger setup for schedules, webhooks, or internal runs.
routines.name: Name
routines.noRoutines: No routines yet. Use Create routine to define the first recurring workflow.
routines.routineCreatedDesc: Add the first trigger to turn it into a live workflow.

routineDetail.selectCompany: Select a company to view routines.
routineDetail.coalesceIfActiveDesc: Keep one follow-up run queued while an active run is still working.
routineDetail.alwaysEnqueueDesc: Queue every trigger occurrence, even if several runs stack up.
routineDetail.skipIfActiveDesc: Drop overlapping trigger occurrences while the routine is already active.
routineDetail.skipMissedDesc: Ignore schedule windows that were missed while the routine or scheduler was paused.
routineDetail.enqueueMissedWithCapDesc: Catch up missed schedule windows in capped batches after recovery.
routineDetail.label: Label
routineDetail.signingModeBearerDesc: Expect a shared bearer token in the Authorization header.
routineDetail.signingModeHmacDesc: Expect an HMAC SHA-256 signature over the request using the shared secret.
routineDetail.saveSecretNow: Save this now. Paperclip will not show the secret value again.

approvals.confirmedDesc: Requesting agent was notified to review this approval and linked issues.
approvals.linkedIssuesNote: Linked issues remain open until the requesting agent follows up and closes them.
approvals.budgetNote: Resolve this budget stop from the budget controls on /costs.
approvals.budgetNotePrefix: Resolve this budget stop from the budget controls on
approvals.deleteConfirm: Delete this disapproved agent? This cannot be undone.

costs.heading: Costs
costs.subtitle: Inference spend, platform fees, credits, and live quota windows.
costs.selectCompany: Select a company to view costs.
costs.financeDesc: Account-level charges that do not map to a single inference request.
costs.netSubtitle: Debit minus credit for the selected period
costs.estimatedSubtitle: Estimated debits that are not yet invoice-authoritative
costs.selectDates: Select a start and end date to load data.
costs.inferenceLedgerDesc: Request-scoped inference spend for the selected period.
costs.byAgentDesc: What each agent consumed in the selected period.
costs.byProjectDesc: Run costs attributed through project-linked issues.
costs.budgetControlPlaneDesc: Hard-stop spend limits for agents and projects. Provider subscription quota stays separate and appears under Providers.
costs.activeIncidentsDesc: Open soft or hard threshold crossings
costs.pendingApprovalsDesc: Budget override approvals awaiting board action
costs.incidentsDesc: Resolve hard stops here by raising the budget or explicitly keeping the scope paused.
costs.budgetEmpty: No budget policies yet. Set agent and project budgets from their detail pages, or use the existing company monthly budget control.
costs.noCostEventsInPeriod: No cost events in this period.
costs.noBillableEvents: No billable events in this period.
costs.byBillerDesc: Account-level financial events grouped by who charged or credited them.
costs.noFinanceEventsTimeline: No finance events yet. Add account-level charges once biller invoices or credits land.
costs.scopeBudgetsAgentDesc: Recurring monthly spend policies for individual agents.
costs.scopeBudgetsProjectDesc: Lifetime spend policies for execution-bound projects.
costs.utilizationDetail: {{percent}}% of monthly budget consumed in this range.

companySettings.title: Company Settings
companySettings.companyNameHint: The display name for your company.
companySettings.descriptionHint: Optional description shown in the company profile.
companySettings.logoHint: Upload a PNG, JPEG, WEBP, GIF, or SVG logo image.
companySettings.brandColorHint: Sets the hue for the company icon. Leave empty for auto-generated color.
companySettings.requireApproval: Require board approval for new hires
companySettings.requireApprovalDesc: New agent hires stay pending until approved by board.
companySettings.generateInviteHint: Creates a short-lived OpenClaw agent invite and renders a copy-ready prompt.
companySettings.archiveDesc: Archive this company to hide it from the sidebar. This persists in the database.
companySettings.archiveConfirm: Archive company "{{name}}"? It will be hidden from the sidebar.
companySettings.noCompanySelected: No company selected. Select a company from the switcher above.
companySettings.inviteDesc: Generate an OpenClaw agent invite snippet.
companySettings.inviteHintFull: Creates a short-lived OpenClaw agent invite and renders a copy-ready prompt.

instanceSettings.heartbeatsDesc: Agents with a timer heartbeat enabled across all of your companies.
instanceSettings.noHeartbeatsCriteria: No scheduler heartbeats match the current criteria.
instanceSettings.experimentalDesc: Opt into features that are still being evaluated before they become default behavior.
instanceSettings.isolatedWorkspacesDesc: Show execution workspace controls in project configuration and allow isolated workspace behavior for new and existing issue runs.

executionWorkspaceDetail.label: Execution workspace
executionWorkspaceDetail.description: Configure the concrete runtime workspace that Paperclip reuses for this issue flow.
executionWorkspaceDetail.descriptionDetail: These settings stay attached to the execution workspace so future runs can keep local paths, repo refs, provisioning, teardown, and runtime-service behavior in sync with the actual workspace being reused.
executionWorkspaceDetail.workspaceSettingsDesc: Edit the concrete path, repo, branch, provisioning, teardown, and runtime overrides attached to this execution workspace.
executionWorkspaceDetail.provisionCommandHint: Runs when Paperclip prepares this execution workspace
executionWorkspaceDetail.teardownCommandHint: Runs when the execution workspace is archived or cleaned up
executionWorkspaceDetail.runtimeConfigSourceExecution: This execution workspace currently overrides the project workspace runtime config.
executionWorkspaceDetail.runtimeConfigSourceProject: This execution workspace is inheriting the project workspace runtime config.
executionWorkspaceDetail.runtimeConfigSourceNone: No runtime config is currently defined on this execution workspace or its project workspace.
executionWorkspaceDetail.runtimeServicesJsonHint: Concrete workspace runtime settings for this execution workspace. Leave this inheriting unless you need a one-off override. If you are missing the right commands, ask your CEO to set them up for you.
executionWorkspaceDetail.noServicesRunning: No runtime services are currently running for this execution workspace.
executionWorkspaceDetail.noRuntimeConfig: No runtime config is defined for this execution workspace yet.
executionWorkspaceDetail.noOperationsRecorded: No workspace operations have been recorded yet.

newAgent.subtitle: Advanced agent configuration

pluginManager.installDialogDesc: Enter the npm package name of the plugin you wish to install.

pluginManager.uninstallDialogDesc: Are you sure you want to uninstall {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.

newAgentDialog.recommendation: We recommend letting your CEO handle agent setup — they know the org structure and can configure reporting, permissions, and adapters.
newAgentDialog.askCeo: Ask the CEO to create a new agent
newAgentDialog.chooseAdapter: Choose your adapter type for advanced setup.

budget.agentPaused: Execution is paused for this project until the budget is raised or the incident is dismissed.
budget.heartbeatsPaused: Heartbeats are paused for this scope until the budget is raised or the incident is dismissed.
budget.invalidAmount: Enter a valid non-negative dollar amount.

onboarding.companyDescription: This is the organization your agents will work for.
onboarding.companyGoalPlaceholder: What is this company trying to achieve?
onboarding.agentDescription: Choose how this agent will run tasks.
onboarding.workingDirectoryHint: Paperclip works best if you create a new folder for your agents to keep their memories and stay organized. Create a new folder and put the path here.
onboarding.adapterEnvironmentDescription: Runs a live probe that asks the adapter CLI to respond with hello.
onboarding.claudeUnsetHint: Claude failed while ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set. You can clear it in this CEO adapter config and retry the probe.
onboarding.authFailsSetEnv: If auth fails, set {{envVar}} in env or run {{command}}.
onboarding.authFailsClaude: If login is required, run claude login and retry.
onboarding.taskDescriptionHeading: Give your agent a small task to start with — a bug fix, a research question, writing a script.
onboarding.taskDescriptionPlaceholder: Add more detail about what the agent should do...
onboarding.launchDescription: Everything is set up. Launching now will create the starter task, wake the agent, and open the issue.
onboarding.emptyStateDescription: Get started by creating a company.
onboarding.runEnvNeedsCompany: Create or select a company before testing adapter environment.
onboarding.openCodeExplicitModel: OpenCode requires an explicit model in provider/model format.
onboarding.openCodeStillLoading: OpenCode models are still loading. Please wait and try again.
onboarding.openCodeNoneDiscovered: No OpenCode models discovered. Run `opencode models` and authenticate providers.
onboarding.unsetAnthropicRetryFailed: Retried with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset in adapter config, but the environment test is still failing.
onboarding.unsetAnthropicFailed: Failed to unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and retry.
onboarding.defaultTaskDescription:
Setup yourself as the CEO. Use the ceo persona found here:

https://github.com/paperclipai/companies/blob/main/default/ceo/AGENTS.md

Ensure you have a folder agents/ceo and then download this AGENTS.md, and sibling HEARTBEAT.md, SOUL.md, and TOOLS.md. and set that AGENTS.md as the path to your agents instruction file

After that, hire yourself a Founding Engineer agent and then plan the roadmap and tasks for your new company.
onboarding.routeAddAnotherAgentDesc: Run onboarding again to add an agent and a starter task for this company.
onboarding.routeCreateAnotherCompanyDesc: Run onboarding again to create another company and seed its first agent.
onboarding.routeCreateFirstCompanyDesc: Get started by creating a company and your first agent.

issuesList.emptyMessage: No issues match the current filters or search.

pluginSettings.author: Author
pluginSettings.noSettings: This plugin does not require any settings.
pluginSettings.runtimeDashboardDesc: Worker process, scheduled jobs, and webhook deliveries
pluginSettings.runtimeUnavailable: Runtime diagnostics are unavailable right now.
pluginSettings.healthChecksWhenReady: Health checks run once the plugin is ready.

pathInstructions.title: Specify path manually
pathInstructions.description: Browser security blocks apps from reading full local paths via a file picker. Copy the absolute path and paste it into the input.
pathInstructions.macosStep2: Hold Option and right-click the folder.
pathInstructions.macosStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
pathInstructions.windowsStep1: Find the folder in File Explorer.
pathInstructions.windowsStep2: Hold Shift and right-click the folder.
pathInstructions.windowsStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
pathInstructions.terminalStep3: Copy the output and paste it into the path input.

newIssueDialog.failedToCreate: Failed to create issue. Try again.
newIssueDialog.executionWorkspaceDescription: Control whether this issue runs in the shared workspace, a new isolated workspace, or an existing one.

projectProperties.executionWorkspacesHelp: Project-owned defaults for isolated issue checkouts and execution workspace behavior.
projectProperties.executionWorkspacesDescription: Let issues choose between the project's primary checkout and an isolated execution workspace.
projectProperties.codebaseHelp: Repo identifies the source of truth. Local folder is the default place agents write code.
projectProperties.additionalLegacyWorkspaceRecords: Additional legacy workspace records exist on this project. Paperclip is using the primary workspace as the codebase view.
projectProperties.clearLocalFolderConfirm: Clear local folder from this workspace?
projectProperties.advancedCheckoutHelp: Provision runs inside the derived worktree before agent execution. Teardown is stored here for future cleanup flows.
projectProperties.archiveDescription: Archive this project to hide it from the sidebar and project selectors.
projectProperties.unarchiveDescription: Unarchive this project to restore it in the sidebar and project selectors.
projectProperties.newIssuesDefaultIsolated: New issues default to isolated checkout
projectProperties.newIssuesDefaultIsolatedHelp: If disabled, new issues stay on the project's primary checkout unless someone opts in.

newProjectDialog.whereWorkWillBeDone: Where will work be done on this project?
newProjectDialog.workspaceDescription: Add a repo and/or local folder for this project.
newProjectDialog.localFolderDescription: Use a full path on this machine.
newProjectDialog.bothDescription: Configure both repo and local folder.
newProjectDialog.localFolderAbsolutePathError: Local folder must be a full absolute path.
newProjectDialog.repoUrlError: Repo must use a valid GitHub repo URL.
newProjectDialog.repoUrlHint: Paste a GitHub repo URL for this project.
newProjectDialog.localFolderHint: Enter the full absolute path to the local folder for this project.

agentConfigForm.selectCompanyToCreateSecrets: Select a company to create secrets
agentConfigForm.selectCompanyToUploadImages: Select a company to upload images
agentConfigForm.selectCompanyToTestEnvironment: Select a company to test adapter environment
agentConfigForm.name: Name
agentConfigForm.title: Title
agentConfigForm.capabilitiesPlaceholder: Describe what this agent can do...
agentConfigForm.promptTemplatePlaceholder: You are agent {{ agent.name }}. Your role is {{ agent.role }}...
agentConfigForm.promptTemplateHint: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Keep it compact and dynamic to avoid recurring token cost and cache churn.
agentConfigForm.promptTemplateCreateHint: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Prefer small task framing and variables like {{ context.* }} or {{ run.* }}; avoid repeating stable instructions here.
agentConfigForm.codexMinimalWarning: Codex may reject minimal thinking when search is enabled.
agentConfigForm.bootstrapPromptPlaceholder: Optional initial setup prompt for the first run
agentConfigForm.bootstrapPromptHint: Bootstrap prompt is only sent for fresh sessions. Put stable setup, habits, and longer reusable guidance here. Frequent changes reduce the value of session reuse because new sessions must replay it.
agentConfigForm.nativeSummaryConfirmed: {{adapter}} is treated as natively managing long context, so Paperclip fresh-session rotation defaults to off.
agentConfigForm.nativeSummaryLikely: {{adapter}} likely manages long context itself, but Paperclip still keeps conservative rotation defaults for now.
agentConfigForm.nativeSummaryUnknown: {{adapter}} does not have verified native compaction behavior, so Paperclip keeps conservative rotation defaults.
agentConfigForm.noFreshSessionThresholds: No Paperclip-managed fresh-session thresholds are active for this adapter.
agentConfigForm.freshSessionThresholds: Paperclip will start a fresh session when one of these thresholds is reached.
agentConfigForm.sessionCompactionNote: A large cumulative raw token total does not mean the full session is resent on every heartbeat.
agentConfigForm.sessionCompactionOverrideNote: This agent has an explicit runtimeConfig session compaction override.
agentConfigForm.createSecretFromCurrentValue: Create secret from current plain value
agentConfigForm.storeValueAsSecret: Store value as secret and replace with reference
agentConfigForm.paperclipEnvVarsHint: PAPERCLIP_* variables are injected automatically at runtime.

issueDocuments.keyFormatHint: Use lowercase letters, numbers, -, or _, and start with a letter or number.
issueDocuments.keyAndBodyRequired: Document key and body are required
issueDocuments.keyValidationError: Document key must start with a letter or number and use only lowercase letters, numbers, -, or _.
issueDocuments.remoteChangedCouldNotLoad: Document changed remotely and the latest version could not be loaded
issueDocuments.outOfDateDescription: This document changed while you were editing. Your local draft is preserved and autosave is paused.
issueDocuments.deleteConfirm: Delete this document? This cannot be undone.

liveRunWidget.description: Streamed with the same transcript UI used on the full run detail page.

budgetIncident.spendingReached: Spending reached {{observed}} against a limit of {{limit}}.
budgetIncident.projectPaused: Project execution is paused. New work in this project will not start until you resolve the budget incident.
budgetIncident.scopePaused: This scope is paused. New heartbeats will not start until you resolve the budget incident.
budgetIncident.budgetMustExceed: The new budget must exceed current observed spend.

accountingModel.title: Accounting model
accountingModel.description: Paperclip now separates request-level inference usage from account-level finance events. That keeps provider reporting honest when the biller is OpenRouter, Cloudflare, Bedrock, or another intermediary.

inferenceLedger.title: Inference ledger
inferenceLedger.description: Request-scoped usage and billed runs from cost_events.

financeLedger.title: Finance ledger
financeLedger.description: Account-level charges that are not one prompt-response pair.

liveQuotas.title: Live quotas
liveQuotas.description: Provider or biller windows that can stop traffic in real time.

agentConfigHelp.name: Display name for this agent.
agentConfigHelp.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
agentConfigHelp.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
agentConfigHelp.reportsTo: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
agentConfigHelp.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
agentConfigHelp.adapterType: How this agent runs: local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP webhook.
agentConfigHelp.cwd: Default working directory fallback for local adapters. Use an absolute path on the machine running Paperclip.
agentConfigHelp.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }} and other template variables.
agentConfigHelp.model: Override the default model used by the adapter.
agentConfigHelp.thinkingEffort: Control model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
agentConfigHelp.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
agentConfigHelp.dangerouslySkipPermissions: Run Claude without permission prompts. Required for unattended operation.
agentConfigHelp.dangerouslyBypassSandbox: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Required for filesystem/network access.
agentConfigHelp.search: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
agentConfigHelp.workspaceStrategy: How Paperclip should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
agentConfigHelp.workspaceBaseRef: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
agentConfigHelp.workspaceBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
agentConfigHelp.worktreeParentDir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
agentConfigHelp.runtimeServicesJson: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
agentConfigHelp.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
agentConfigHelp.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
agentConfigHelp.localCommand: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
agentConfigHelp.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
agentConfigHelp.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
agentConfigHelp.bootstrapPrompt: Only sent when Paperclip starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
agentConfigHelp.payloadTemplateJson: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Paperclip adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
agentConfigHelp.webhookUrl: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
agentConfigHelp.heartbeatInterval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
agentConfigHelp.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
agentConfigHelp.graceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
agentConfigHelp.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
agentConfigHelp.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
agentConfigHelp.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
agentConfigHelp.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.
agentConfigHelp.fastMode: Enable Codex Fast mode. This burns credits/tokens much faster and is currently supported on GPT-5.4 only.

adapterConfigFields.instructionsFileHintSystem: Absolute path to a markdown file (for example AGENTS.md) that defines this agent's behavior. Injected into the system prompt at runtime.
adapterConfigFields.instructionsFileHintPrompt: Absolute path to a markdown file (for example AGENTS.md) that defines this agent's behavior. Injected into the prompt at runtime.
adapterConfigFields.instructionsFileHintGemini: Absolute path to a markdown file (for example AGENTS.md) that defines this agent's behavior. Prepended to the Gemini prompt at runtime.
adapterConfigFields.deviceAuthDescription: Always enabled for gateway agents. Paperclip persists a device key during onboarding so pairing approvals remain stable across runs.

org.selectCompany: Select a company to view org chart.
org.noAgents: No agents in the organization. Create agents to build your org chart.

cliAuth.accessApprovedDescription: The Paperclip CLI can now finish authentication on the requesting machine.
cliAuth.startFlowAgain: Start the CLI auth flow again from your terminal to generate a new approval request.
cliAuth.signInRequiredDescription: Sign in or create an account, then return to this page to approve the CLI access request.
cliAuth.approveDescription: A local Paperclip CLI process is requesting board access to this instance.
cliAuth.failedToUpdate: Failed to update CLI auth challenge
cliAuth.requiresInstanceAdmin: This challenge requires instance-admin access. Sign in with an instance admin account to approve it.

instanceGeneralSettings.description: Configure instance-wide defaults that affect how operator-visible logs are displayed.
instanceGeneralSettings.censorUsernameDescription: Hide the username segment in home-directory paths and similar operator-visible log output. Standalone username mentions outside of paths are not yet masked in the live transcript view. This is off by default.

pluginPage.selectCompany: Select a company to view this page.
pluginPage.multiplePluginsConflict: Multiple plugins declare the route {{route}}. Use the plugin-id route until the conflict is resolved.

companyImport.importSourceDescription: Choose a GitHub repo or upload a local Paperclip zip package.
companyImport.localZipHelpText: Upload a .zip exported directly from Paperclip. Re-zipped archives created by Finder, Explorer, or other zip tools may not import correctly.
companyImport.githubUrlHint: Repo tree path or blob URL to COMPANY.md (e.g. github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/company).

adapterManager.installExternalAdapterDesc: Add an adapter from npm or a local path. The adapter package must export createServerAdapter().
adapterManager.localPathHint: Accepts Linux, WSL, and Windows paths. Windows paths are auto-converted.
adapterManager.externalAdaptersAlphaDesc: The adapter plugin system is under active development. APIs and storage format may change. Use the power icon to hide adapters from agent menus without removing them.
adapterManager.noExternalAdaptersHint: Install an adapter package to extend model support.
adapterManager.removeAdapterDesc: Are you sure you want to remove the {{type}} adapter? It will be unregistered and removed from the adapter store.
adapterManager.removeAdapterNpmCleanup: npm packages will be cleaned up from disk.
adapterManager.reinstallAdapterDesc: This will pull the latest version of {{package}} from npm and hot-swap the running adapter module. Existing agents will use the new version on their next run.

projectWorkspaceDetail.pageDescription: Configure this workspace's source, runtime services, and access settings.
projectWorkspaceDetail.isPrimaryDescription: This is the primary workspace for the project.

localPath.label: Local git checkout
localPath.description: A local path Paperclip can use directly.

nonGitPath.label: Local non-git path
nonGitPath.description: A local path that is not a git repository.

gitRepo.label: Remote git repo
gitRepo.description: A repo URL with optional refs and local checkout.

remoteManaged.label: Remote managed
remoteManaged.description: A remotely managed workspace (e.g. GitHub Codespaces).

hints.setupCommand: Runs when this workspace needs custom bootstrap
hints.cleanupCommand: Runs when this workspace is being torn down
hints.runtimeServicesJson: Defines long-running services (e.g. dev servers) to start alongside agents

runtime.heading: Runtime
runtime.description: Services that run alongside agents using this workspace.
runtime.noServicesRunning: No services running. Use the Start button to launch them.
runtime.noDefaultConfigured: No default runtime config. Add a runtime services JSON config above and save.

errors.remoteManagedRequiresRef: Remote managed workspaces require a remote workspace ref or repo URL.
errors.requiresPathOrUrl: A local path or repo URL is required.
errors.localPathMustBeAbsolute: Local path must be an absolute path.

errors.runtimeJsonMustBeObject: Runtime config must be a JSON object.
